1	name: Sync PR state to Linear
2	
3	on:
4	  pull_request:
5	    types: [opened, reopened, ready_for_review, closed]
6	
7	jobs:
8	  sync:
9	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
10	    steps:
11	      - name: Sync matching Linear issue
12	        env:
13	          LINEAR_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.LINEAR_API_KEY }}
14	          PR_TITLE: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}
15	          PR_MERGED: ${{ github.event.pull_request.merged }}
16	          PR_ACTION: ${{ github.event.action }}
17	        run: |
18	          python3 - <<'PY'
19	          import json
20	          import os
21	          import re
22	          import sys
23	          import urllib.request
24	
25	          api_key = os.getenv("LINEAR_API_KEY")
26	          if not api_key:
27	            print("LINEAR_API_KEY is not set.")
28	            sys.exit(1)
29	
30	          pr_title = os.getenv("PR_TITLE", "")
31	          action = os.getenv("PR_ACTION", "")
32	          merged = os.getenv("PR_MERGED", "false").lower() == "true"
33	
34	          # Keep this strict/safe: only act when the PR title contains a Linear key.
35	          match = re.search(r"\bLAF-\d+\b", pr_title)
36	          if not match:
37	            print(f"No Linear issue identifier found in PR title: {pr_title!r}. Skipping.")
38	            sys.exit(0)
39	
40	          identifier = match.group(0)
41	          if action == "closed":
42	            if not merged:
43	              print("PR was closed without merge. Skipping.")
44	              sys.exit(0)
45	            target_state_type = "completed"
46	            target_state_name = "Done"
47	          else:
48	            target_state_type = "started"
49	            target_state_name = "In Progress"
50	
51	          def linear_graphql(query: str, variables: dict):
52	            payload = json.dumps({"query": query, "variables": variables}).encode("utf-8")
53	            req = urllib.request.Request(
54	              "https://api.linear.app/graphql",
55	              data=payload,
56	              headers={
57	                "Authorization": api_key,
58	                "Content-Type": "application/json",
59	              },
60	              method="POST",
61	            )
62	            with urllib.request.urlopen(req) as resp:
63	              body = json.loads(resp.read().decode("utf-8"))
64	            if "errors" in body and body["errors"]:
65	              raise RuntimeError(f"Linear API error: {body['errors']}")
66	            return body["data"]
67	
68	          issue_query = """
69	          query IssueByIdentifier($identifier: String!) {
70	            issues(filter: { identifier: { eq: $identifier } }) {
71	              nodes {
72	                id
73	                identifier
74	                team {
75	                  id
76	                  key
77	                  name
78	                  states {
79	                    nodes {
80	                      id
81	                      name
82	                      type
83	                    }
84	                  }
85	                }
86	              }
87	            }
88	          }
89	          """
90	          issue_data = linear_graphql(issue_query, {"identifier": identifier})
91	          issues = issue_data.get("issues", {}).get("nodes", [])
92	          if not issues:
93	            print(f"Linear issue {identifier} was not found.")
94	            sys.exit(1)
95	
96	          issue = issues[0]
97	          states = issue["team"]["states"]["nodes"]
98	
99	          # Prefer state `type` so IDs come from the real team workflow.
100	          target_state = next((s for s in states if s.get("type") == target_state_type), None)
101	          if not target_state:
102	            target_state = next((s for s in states if s.get("name", "").lower() == target_state_name.lower()), None)
103	
104	          if not target_state:
105	            print(
106	              f"Could not find a '{target_state_name}' state for team {issue['team']['key']} "
107	              f"(looked for type='{target_state_type}' and name='{target_state_name}')."
108	            )
109	            sys.exit(1)
110	
111	          update_mutation = """
112	          mutation UpdateIssueState($issueId: String!, $stateId: String!) {
113	            issueUpdate(id: $issueId, input: { stateId: $stateId }) {
114	              success
115	            }
116	          }
117	          """
118	          update_data = linear_graphql(
119	            update_mutation,
120	            {"issueId": issue["id"], "stateId": target_state["id"]},
121	          )
122	          success = update_data.get("issueUpdate", {}).get("success")
123	          if not success:
124	            print(f"Failed to update issue {identifier} to {target_state['name']}.")
125	            sys.exit(1)
126	
127	          print(f"Updated {identifier} to '{target_state['name']}'.")
128	          PY
129

1	name: Linear Brief Generator
2	
3	on:
4	  repository_dispatch:
5	    types: [linear-brief]
6	
7	  workflow_dispatch:
8	    inputs:
9	      payload:
10	        description: 'Linear issue JSON payload (for manual testing)'
11	        required: true
12	        type: string
13	      live_write:
14	        description: 'Opt in to writing files and opening a PR'
15	        required: false
16	        default: false
17	        type: boolean
18	
19	jobs:
20	  generate-brief:
21	    name: Generate research brief
22	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
23	
24	    permissions:
25	      contents: write
26	      pull-requests: write
27	
28	    steps:
29	      - name: Checkout vault
30	        uses: actions/checkout@v6
31	
32	      - name: Setup vault environment
33	        uses: ./.github/actions/setup-vault
34	        with:
35	          python-version: "3.11"
36	          pip-packages: "anthropic openai PyYAML>=6.0"
37	
38	      - name: Validate dispatch trust boundary and write mode
39	        id: guardrails
40	        env:
41	          EVENT_NAME: ${{ github.event_name }}
42	          LINEAR_PAYLOAD: ${{ github.event.client_payload && toJson(github.event.client_payload) || github.event.inputs.payload }}
43	          WORKFLOW_LIVE_WRITE: ${{ github.event.inputs.live_write || 'false' }}
44	          LINEAR_BRIEF_WEBHOOK_SECRET: ${{ secrets.LINEAR_BRIEF_WEBHOOK_SECRET }}
45	        run: |
46	          python3 - <<'PY'
47	          import hmac
48	          import json
49	          import os
50	          import sys
51	
52	          event_name = os.environ["EVENT_NAME"]
53	          payload = json.loads(os.environ["LINEAR_PAYLOAD"])
54	          workflow_live_write = os.environ.get("WORKFLOW_LIVE_WRITE", "false").lower() == "true"
55	          configured_secret = os.environ.get("LINEAR_BRIEF_WEBHOOK_SECRET", "")
56	
57	          live_write = workflow_live_write
58	          if event_name == "repository_dispatch":
59	            payload_live_write = payload.get("live_write", False)
60	            if isinstance(payload_live_write, str):
61	              payload_live_write = payload_live_write.lower() == "true"
62	            live_write = bool(payload_live_write)
63	
64	            provided_secret = payload.get("webhook_secret", "")
65	            if configured_secret:
66	              if not isinstance(provided_secret, str) or not hmac.compare_digest(provided_secret, configured_secret):
67	                print("repository_dispatch payload failed webhook secret validation.", file=sys.stderr)
68	                sys.exit(1)
69	            elif live_write:
70	              print(
71	                "Refusing live repository_dispatch write without LINEAR_BRIEF_WEBHOOK_SECRET configured.",
72	                file=sys.stderr,
73	              )
74	              sys.exit(1)
75	
76	          github_output = os.environ["GITHUB_OUTPUT"]
77	          with open(github_output, "a", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
78	            fh.write(f"live_write={'true' if live_write else 'false'}\n")
79	
80	          print(f"Write mode: {'live' if live_write else 'dry-run'}")
81	          PY
82	
83	      - name: Generate brief
84	        id: generate
85	        env:
86	          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
87	          OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}
88	          OPENAI_MODEL: gpt-5.4
89	          OPENAI_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS: "4096"
90	          LINEAR_PAYLOAD: ${{ github.event.client_payload && toJson(github.event.client_payload) || github.event.inputs.payload }}
91	        run: |
92	          ARGS=()
93	          if [ "${{ steps.guardrails.outputs.live_write }}" != "true" ]; then
94	            ARGS+=("--dry-run")
95	          fi
96	          python3 .github/scripts/linear_brief_generator.py "${ARGS[@]}"
97	
98	          # Capture the Linear ID for branch naming
99	          LINEAR_ID=$(echo "$LINEAR_PAYLOAD" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('id', d.get('identifier', 'unknown')))")
100	          echo "linear_id=$LINEAR_ID" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
101	          echo "date=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%d)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
102	
103	      - name: Validate generated content
104	        if: steps.guardrails.outputs.live_write == 'true'
105	        run: |
106	          git add "!/"
107	          python3 .github/scripts/validate_content.py --scope generated
108	          git reset HEAD
109	
110	      - name: Create branch and PR
111	        if: steps.guardrails.outputs.live_write == 'true'
112	        env:
113	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
114	        run: |
115	          LINEAR_ID="${{ steps.generate.outputs.linear_id }}"
116	          DATE="${{ steps.generate.outputs.date }}"
117	          BRANCH="briefs/${LINEAR_ID}-${DATE}"
118	
119	          git checkout -b "$BRANCH"
120	          git add "!/"
121	
122	          if git diff --cached --quiet; then
123	            echo "No changes to commit."
124	            exit 0
125	          fi
126	
127	          TITLE=$(echo "${{ env.LINEAR_PAYLOAD }}" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('title','Untitled'))" 2>/dev/null || echo "Untitled")
128	          git commit -m "brief: ${TITLE} — ${DATE}"
129	          git push -u origin "$BRANCH"
130	
131	          gh pr create \
132	            --title "Brief: ${TITLE}" \
133	            --body "$(cat <<EOF
134	          ## Research Brief
135	
136	          Generated from Linear issue \`${LINEAR_ID}\`.
137	
138	          **Review the brief in \`!/BRIEF-${LINEAR_ID}-${DATE}.md\`**, then:
139	          - Merge to land it in \`!/\` (the mailroom)
140	          - Move the file to the appropriate vault location after merge
141	
142	          ---
143	          _Generated by Stage 1 Mesh pipeline_
144	          EOF
145	          )" \
146	            --base main \
147	            --head "$BRANCH"
148

1	name: Auto PR for Agent Branches
2	
3	on:
4	  push:
5	    branches:
6	      - 'claude/**'
7	      - 'codex/**'
8	      - 'gemini/**'
9	      - 'copilot/**'
10	      - 'perplexity/**'
11	      - 'grok/**'
12	
13	jobs:
14	  auto-pr:
15	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
16	    permissions:
17	      contents: write
18	      pull-requests: write
19	
20	    steps:
21	      - name: Checkout repo
22	        uses: actions/checkout@v6
23	        with:
24	          fetch-depth: 0
25	
26	      - name: Get changed files and classify
27	        id: classify
28	        run: |
29	          CHANGED=$(git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD)
30	          if [ -z "$CHANGED" ]; then
31	            echo "No changes vs main"
32	            echo "skip=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
33	            exit 0
34	          fi
35	          echo "skip=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
36	
37	          RESULT=$(echo "$CHANGED" | python3 .github/scripts/classify_paths.py)
38	          TIER=$(echo "$RESULT" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['tier'])")
39	          echo "tier=$TIER" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
40	          echo "result=$RESULT" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
41	
42	          echo "### Changed files" >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
43	          echo "$CHANGED" >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
44	          echo "### Risk tier: $TIER" >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
45	
46	      - name: Check for existing PR
47	        if: steps.classify.outputs.skip != 'true'
48	        id: check-pr
49	        env:
50	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
51	        run: |
52	          BRANCH="${GITHUB_REF_NAME}"
53	          EXISTING=$(gh pr list --head "$BRANCH" --state open --json number --jq '.[0].number // empty')
54	          if [ -n "$EXISTING" ]; then
55	            echo "pr_exists=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
56	            echo "pr_number=$EXISTING" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
57	          else
58	            echo "pr_exists=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
59	          fi
60	
61	      - name: Ensure labels exist
62	        if: steps.classify.outputs.skip != 'true' && steps.check-pr.outputs.pr_exists != 'true'
63	        env:
64	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
65	        run: |
66	          gh label create "auto-merge" --color "0E8A16" --description "Low-risk, eligible for auto-merge" --force 2>/dev/null || true
67	          gh label create "review-required" --color "D93F0B" --description "High-risk, requires Logan's review" --force 2>/dev/null || true
68	
69	      - name: Create PR
70	        if: steps.classify.outputs.skip != 'true' && steps.check-pr.outputs.pr_exists != 'true'
71	        id: create-pr
72	        env:
73	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
74	          RISK_TIER: ${{ steps.classify.outputs.tier }}
75	        run: |
76	          BRANCH="${GITHUB_REF_NAME}"
77	          # Derive title: strip agent prefix (claude/, codex/, gemini/, etc.) and session suffix
78	          TITLE=$(echo "$BRANCH" | sed 's|^[^/]*/||' | sed 's|-[a-zA-Z0-9]*$||' | tr '-' ' ')
79	          # Extract agent name from branch prefix
80	          AGENT=$(echo "$BRANCH" | sed 's|/.*||')
81	          TIER="$RISK_TIER"
82	          DIFFSTAT=$(git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD)
83	
84	          if [ "$TIER" = "low" ]; then
85	            LABEL="auto-merge"
86	          else
87	            LABEL="review-required"
88	          fi
89	
90	          # Build PR body
91	          printf -v BODY '%s\n\n%s\n%s\n%s\n%s\n\n%s\n%s\n%s\n%s\n\n%s\n%s' \
92	            '## Auto-generated PR' \
93	            '**Agent:**' "\`$AGENT\`" \
94	            '**Risk tier:**' "\`$TIER\`" \
95	            '**Label:**' "\`$LABEL\`" \
96	            '### Diff stat' \
97	            '```' "$DIFFSTAT" '```' \
98	            '---' '*Created by auto-pr workflow*'
99	
100	          PR_URL=$(gh pr create \
101	            --title "$TITLE" \
102	            --body "$BODY" \
103	            --base main \
104	            --head "$BRANCH" \
105	            --label "$LABEL" 2>&1 || true)
106	
107	          echo "pr_url=$PR_URL" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
108	          echo "Created PR: $PR_URL"
109	
110	      - name: Enable auto-merge if low risk
111	        if: steps.classify.outputs.skip != 'true' && steps.classify.outputs.tier == 'low'
112	        env:
113	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
114	        run: |
115	          BRANCH="${GITHUB_REF_NAME}"
116	
117	          # Re-classify as defense in depth
118	          CHANGED=$(git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD)
119	          TIER=$(echo "$CHANGED" | python3 .github/scripts/classify_paths.py | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['tier'])")
120	
121	          if [ "$TIER" != "low" ]; then
122	            echo "Reclassified as high-risk on re-check. Skipping auto-merge."
123	            exit 0
124	          fi
125	
126	          # Find the PR number
127	          PR_NUM=$(gh pr list --head "$BRANCH" --state open --json number --jq '.[0].number')
128	          if [ -z "$PR_NUM" ]; then
129	            echo "No open PR found to merge"
130	            exit 0
131	          fi
132	
133	          echo "Enabling auto-merge for PR #$PR_NUM (low risk)"
134	          gh pr merge "$PR_NUM" --squash --delete-branch --auto
135

1	name: Setup Vault Environment
2	description: >
3	  Shared setup for vault workflows: configures git bot identity,
4	  installs Python + pip dependencies, and caches pip packages.
5	  Use this instead of repeating setup steps in every workflow.
6	
7	inputs:
8	  python-version:
9	    description: Python version to install (skip if empty)
10	    required: false
11	    default: ""
12	  pip-packages:
13	    description: Space-separated pip packages to install (skip if empty)
14	    required: false
15	    default: ""
16	  requirements-file:
17	    description: Path to requirements file for pip install + cache key
18	    required: false
19	    default: ""
20	
21	runs:
22	  using: composite
23	  steps:
24	    - name: Configure git bot identity
25	      shell: bash
26	      run: |
27	        git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
28	        git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
29	
30	    - name: Set up Python
31	      if: inputs.python-version != ''
32	      uses: actions/setup-python@v6
33	      with:
34	        python-version: ${{ inputs.python-version }}
35	        cache: ${{ inputs.requirements-file != '' && 'pip' || '' }}
36	        cache-dependency-path: ${{ inputs.requirements-file }}
37	
38	    - name: Install pip packages (inline)
39	      if: inputs.pip-packages != ''
40	      shell: bash
41	      run: pip install ${{ inputs.pip-packages }}
42	
43	    - name: Install pip packages (requirements file)
44	      if: inputs.requirements-file != ''
45	      shell: bash
46	      run: pip install -r ${{ inputs.requirements-file }}
47

1	name: Branch Cleanup
2	
3	on:
4	  pull_request:
5	    types: [closed]
6	  schedule:
7	    - cron: '0 9 * * 1'  # Every Monday at 9am UTC
8	  workflow_dispatch:
9	
10	jobs:
11	  cleanup-merged:
12	    if: >
13	      github.event_name == 'schedule' ||
14	      github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' ||
15	      (github.event.pull_request.merged == true &&
16	       startsWith(github.event.pull_request.head.ref, 'claude/'))
17	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
18	    permissions:
19	      contents: write
20	
21	    steps:
22	      - name: Checkout repo
23	        uses: actions/checkout@v6
24	        with:
25	          fetch-depth: 0
26	
27	      - name: Delete merged claude branches
28	        env:
29	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
30	          EVENT_NAME: ${{ github.event_name }}
31	          PR_HEAD_REF: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
32	        run: |
33	          # On PR merge, delete the specific branch
34	          if [ "$EVENT_NAME" = "pull_request" ]; then
35	            BRANCH="$PR_HEAD_REF"
36	            echo "Deleting merged branch: $BRANCH"
37	            git push origin --delete "$BRANCH" 2>/dev/null || echo "Branch already deleted"
38	            exit 0
39	          fi
40	
41	          # On schedule/manual: sweep all merged claude/* branches
42	          echo "Scanning for merged claude/* branches..."
43	          git fetch --prune origin
44	
45	          for ref in $(git branch -r --merged origin/main | grep 'origin/claude/' | sed 's|origin/||'); do
46	            echo "Deleting merged branch: $ref"

1	name: Budget Tracker CSV Export
2	
3	# NOTE: This workflow exports bill data to CSV for the Flourish Budget Tracker.
4	# Current status: Idaho Legislature scraper is functional and produces minidata CSV files.
5	# LSO direct access: When minidata.csv files are received via email, they can be manually
6	# triaged and imported into the vault. The scraper provides an alternative data source
7	# when direct LSO access is not available.
8	
9	on:
10	  # Run daily at 6:30 AM Mountain Time (13:30 UTC) - 30 minutes after bill scraper
11	  schedule:
12	    - cron: "30 13 * * *"
13	
14	  # Triggerable from GitHub mobile → Actions tab → Run workflow
15	  workflow_dispatch:
16	    inputs:
17	      year:
18	        description: "Session year to export"
19	        required: false
20	        default: "2026"
21	        type: string
22	
23	jobs:
24	  export-csv:
25	    name: Export Bills to CSV for Flourish
26	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
27	
28	    permissions:
29	      contents: write
30	
31	    steps:
32	      - name: Checkout vault
33	        uses: actions/checkout@v6
34	        with:
35	          fetch-depth: 1
36	
37	      - name: Set up Python
38	        uses: actions/setup-python@v6
39	        with:
40	          python-version: "3.11"
41	          cache: pip
42	          cache-dependency-path: .github/scripts/requirements-scraper.txt
43	
44	      - name: Install dependencies
45	        run: pip install -r .github/scripts/requirements-scraper.txt
46	
47	      - name: Generate CSV export
48	        id: export
49	        # continue-on-error: the export may fail when the Idaho Legislature website is
50	        # unreachable or has a structure change. Downstream steps use the custom
51	        # `export_ok` output variable to guard upload/email, so the job still succeeds
52	        # and the summary shows an informative message instead of a hard failure.
53	        continue-on-error: true
54	        env:
55	          YEAR: ${{ github.event.inputs.year || '2026' }}
56	        run: |
57	          TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%d)
58	          OUTPUT_FILE="minidata-${TIMESTAMP}.csv"
59	
60	          python3 .github/scripts/idaho_leg_scraper.py \
61	            --year "$YEAR" \
62	            --csv-export "$OUTPUT_FILE"
63	
64	          if [ -f "$OUTPUT_FILE" ]; then
65	            BILL_COUNT=$(tail -n +2 "$OUTPUT_FILE" | wc -l)
66	            FILE_SIZE=$(du -h "$OUTPUT_FILE" | cut -f1)
67	            echo "csv_file=$OUTPUT_FILE" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
68	            echo "bill_count=$BILL_COUNT" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
69	            echo "file_size=$FILE_SIZE" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
70	            echo "export_ok=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
71	            echo "CSV export succeeded: $BILL_COUNT bills exported"
72	          else
73	            echo "export_ok=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
74	            echo "CSV export failed — bill list unavailable (site structure change or network issue)"
75	            exit 1
76	          fi
77	
78	      - name: Upload CSV as artifact
79	        if: steps.export.outputs.export_ok == 'true'
80	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
81	        with:
82	          name: minidata-csv-${{ steps.export.outputs.csv_file }}
83	          path: ${{ steps.export.outputs.csv_file }}
84	          retention-days: 90
85	
86	      - name: Email CSV file
87	        if: steps.export.outputs.export_ok == 'true'
88	        uses: dawidd6/action-send-mail@v16
89	        with:
90	          server_address: smtp.gmail.com
91	          server_port: 587
92	          username: ${{ secrets.MAIL_USERNAME }}
93	          password: ${{ secrets.MAIL_PASSWORD }}
94	          subject: "Idaho Legislature CSV Export - ${{ steps.export.outputs.csv_file }}"
95	          to: ${{ secrets.MAIL_TO }}
96	          from: Idaho Vault Automation <${{ secrets.MAIL_USERNAME }}>
97	          body: |
98	            Daily Idaho Legislature bill data export for Flourish Budget Tracker.
99	
100	            Session Year: ${{ github.event.inputs.year || '2026' }}
101	            Bills Exported: ${{ steps.export.outputs.bill_count }}
102	            File Size: ${{ steps.export.outputs.file_size }}
103	            Generated: ${{ steps.export.outputs.csv_file }}
104	
105	            This CSV file can be imported directly into your Flourish budget tracker visualization.
106	
107	            ---
108	            Generated by: ${{ github.repository }}
109	            Workflow: ${{ github.workflow }}
110	            Run: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}
111	          attachments: ${{ steps.export.outputs.csv_file }}
112	
113	      - name: Create GitHub Actions summary
114	        if: always()
115	        env:
116	          EXPORT_OK: ${{ steps.export.outputs.export_ok }}
117	          CSV_FILE: ${{ steps.export.outputs.csv_file }}
118	          BILL_COUNT: ${{ steps.export.outputs.bill_count }}
119	          FILE_SIZE: ${{ steps.export.outputs.file_size }}
120	          SESSION_YEAR: ${{ github.event.inputs.year || '2026' }}
121	        run: |
122	          {
123	            echo "# Budget Tracker CSV Export"
124	            echo ""
125	            echo "**Session Year:** ${SESSION_YEAR}"
126	            if [ "$EXPORT_OK" = "true" ]; then
127	              echo "**Generated:** \`${CSV_FILE}\`"
128	              echo "**Bills Exported:** ${BILL_COUNT}"
129	              echo "**File Size:** ${FILE_SIZE}"
130	              echo ""
131	              echo "The CSV file has been:"
132	              echo "- ✅ Uploaded as a workflow artifact (90-day retention)"
133	              echo "- 📧 Emailed to configured recipient"
134	              echo "- 📊 Ready for Flourish Budget Tracker import"
135	            else
136	              echo ""
137	              echo "⚠️ **Export failed** — could not fetch bill list from Idaho Legislature website."
138	              echo "This may be a transient network issue or a site structure change."
139	              echo "The workflow will retry at the next scheduled run."
140	            fi
141	          } >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
142

1	name: Daily To-Do Rollover
2	
3	# Carries incomplete to-do items from yesterday's daily note into today's.
4	# Runs at 4 AM MT (10:00 UTC) — a liminal hour. See [[The 4 a.m. Mystery]] by [[Rives]].
5	# Can also be triggered manually from the Actions tab.
6	
7	on:
8	  schedule:
9	    - cron: '0 10 * * *'  # 4 AM MT — see [[The 4 a.m. Mystery]] by [[Rives]]
10	  workflow_dispatch:
11	    inputs:
12	      date:
13	        description: 'Target date to roll INTO (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to today.'
14	        required: false
15	        default: ''
16	      dry_run:
17	        description: 'Dry run — print output without writing files'
18	        required: false
19	        default: 'false'
20	        type: boolean
21	
22	jobs:
23	  rollover:
24	    name: Roll over incomplete to-dos
25	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
26	    permissions:
27	      contents: write
28	
29	    steps:
30	      - name: Checkout vault
31	        uses: actions/checkout@v6
32	        with:
33	          fetch-depth: 1
34	
35	      - name: Set up Python
36	        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
37	        with:
38	          python-version: '3.11'
39	
40	      - name: Run daily rollover
41	        run: |
42	          ARGS=()
43	          DATE="${{ github.event.inputs.date }}"
44	          DRY="${{ github.event.inputs.dry_run }}"
45	          [ -n "$DATE" ] && ARGS+=("--date" "$DATE")
46	          [ "$DRY" = "true" ] && ARGS+=("--dry-run")
47	          python3 .github/scripts/daily_rollover.py "${ARGS[@]}"
48	
49	      - name: Commit rollover
50	        run: |
51	          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
52	          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
53	
54	          TARGET_DATE="${{ github.event.inputs.date }}"
55	          [ -z "$TARGET_DATE" ] && TARGET_DATE="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%d)"
56	
57	          git add "${TARGET_DATE}.md" "TO DO LIST.md" 2>/dev/null || true
58	
59	          if git diff --cached --quiet; then
60	            echo "No changes to commit — to-dos already current."
61	          else
62	            git commit -m "rollover: carry incomplete to-dos to ${TARGET_DATE}"
63	            git push
64	          fi

1	name: Idaho Legislature Scraper
2	
3	on:
4	  # Run every day at 6 AM Mountain Time (13:00 UTC)
5	  # TEMPORARILY DISABLED: Pending direct data access from LSO
6	  # schedule:
7	  #   - cron: "0 13 * * *"
8	
9	  # Triggerable from GitHub mobile → Actions tab → Run workflow
10	  workflow_dispatch:
11	    inputs:
12	      year:
13	        description: "Session year to scrape"
14	        required: false
15	        default: "2026"
16	        type: string
17	      force:
18	        description: "Force re-fetch all bills (including finalized)"
19	        required: false
20	        default: "false"
21	        type: boolean
22	      bill:
23	        description: "Scrape only one bill (e.g. H0001, S0042). Leave blank for all."
24	        required: false
25	        default: ""
26	        type: string
27	      members:
28	        description: "Also scrape House/Senate member profiles"
29	        required: false
30	        default: "false"
31	        type: boolean
32	
33	jobs:
34	  scrape:
35	    name: Scrape Idaho Legislature
36	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
37	
38	    permissions:
39	      contents: write
40	      issues: write    # needed to post digest comments
41	
42	    steps:
43	      - name: Checkout vault
44	        uses: actions/checkout@v6
45	        with:
46	          fetch-depth: 1
47	
48	      - name: Setup vault environment
49	        uses: ./.github/actions/setup-vault
50	        with:
51	          python-version: "3.11"
52	          requirements-file: .github/scripts/requirements-scraper.txt
53	
54	      - name: Run scraper
55	        id: scrape
56	        continue-on-error: true   # site may be temporarily unavailable or blocking
57	        env:
58	          YEAR:    ${{ github.event.inputs.year    || '2026' }}
59	          FORCE:   ${{ github.event.inputs.force   || 'false' }}
60	          BILL:    ${{ github.event.inputs.bill     || '' }}
61	          MEMBERS: ${{ github.event.inputs.members  || 'false' }}
62	        run: |
63	          ARGS=("--year" "$YEAR")
64	          [ "$FORCE"   = "true" ] && ARGS+=("--force")
65	          [ -n "$BILL" ]          && ARGS+=("--bill" "$BILL")
66	          [ "$MEMBERS" = "true" ] && ARGS+=("--members")
67	
68	          python3 .github/scripts/idaho_leg_scraper.py "${ARGS[@]}"
69	
70	          # Capture which bill files changed for the digest
71	          git diff --name-only HEAD \
72	            "GOVERNMENTS/IDAHO - LEGISLATIVE/BILLS/" 2>/dev/null \
73	            | grep '\.md$' > /tmp/changed_bills.txt || true
74	
75	          git diff --name-only HEAD \
76	            "GOVERNMENTS/IDAHO - LEGISLATIVE/BILLS/" --diff-filter=A 2>/dev/null \
77	            | grep '\.md$' > /tmp/new_bills.txt || true
78	
79	          echo "changed=$(wc -l < /tmp/changed_bills.txt)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
80	          echo "new=$(wc -l < /tmp/new_bills.txt)"         >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
81	
82	      - name: Validate scraped content
83	        run: |
84	          git add "GOVERNMENTS/IDAHO - LEGISLATIVE/BILLS/"
85	          git add "GOVERNMENTS/IDAHO - LEGISLATIVE/SESSIONS/"
86	          git add "GOVERNMENTS/IDAHO - LEGISLATIVE/IDAHO HOUSE/"
87	          git add "GOVERNMENTS/IDAHO - LEGISLATIVE/IDAHO SENATE/"
88	          python3 .github/scripts/validate_content.py --scope bills
89	          git reset HEAD  # unstage so commit step handles staging
90	
91	      - name: Commit changes
92	        id: commit
93	        if: steps.scrape.outcome == 'success'
94	        run: |
95	          git add "GOVERNMENTS/IDAHO - LEGISLATIVE/BILLS/"
96	          git add "GOVERNMENTS/IDAHO - LEGISLATIVE/SESSIONS/"
97	          git add "GOVERNMENTS/IDAHO - LEGISLATIVE/IDAHO HOUSE/"
98	          git add "GOVERNMENTS/IDAHO - LEGISLATIVE/IDAHO SENATE/"
99	
100	          if git diff --cached --quiet; then
101	            echo "committed=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
102	            echo "No changes to commit."
103	          else
104	            CHANGED=$(git diff --cached --name-only | wc -l)
105	            git commit -m "scrape Idaho legislature — ${CHANGED} file(s) $(date -u +%Y-%m-%d)"
106	            git push
107	            echo "committed=true"  >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
108	            echo "file_count=${CHANGED}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
109	          fi
110	
111	      - name: Post digest comment to tracking issue
112	        if: steps.commit.outputs.committed == 'true'
113	        env:
114	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
115	          YEAR:     ${{ github.event.inputs.year || '2026' }}
116	        run: |
117	          python3 .github/scripts/post_digest.py \
118	            --changed-file /tmp/changed_bills.txt \
119	            --new-file     /tmp/new_bills.txt \
120	            --year         "$YEAR"

15	      github.event.pull_request.user.login != github.event.review.user.login

19	        uses: actions/checkout@v4

22	        env:
23	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
24	          REVIEWER: ${{ github.event.review.user.login }}
25	          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
26	          REVIEW_ID: ${{ github.event.review.id }}
27	          REVIEW_STATE: ${{ github.event.review.state }}
28	        run: |
29	          gh label create "review-required" --color "D93F0B" --description "High-risk, requires Logan's review" --force 2>/dev/null || true
30	
31	          REVIEW_URL="${GITHUB_SERVER_URL}/${GITHUB_REPOSITORY}/pull/${PR_NUMBER}#pullrequestreview-${REVIEW_ID}"
32	
33	          # Pause auto-merge until feedback is addressed
34	          gh pr merge "$PR_NUMBER" --disable-auto 2>/dev/null || true
35	          gh pr edit "$PR_NUMBER" --remove-label "auto-merge" 2>/dev/null || true
36	          gh pr edit "$PR_NUMBER" --add-label "review-required" 2>/dev/null || true
37	
38	          gh pr comment "$PR_NUMBER" --body "Acknowledged review by @$REVIEWER (${REVIEW_STATE}). Auto-merge paused until recommended changes are addressed. [Review link]($REVIEW_URL)"

6	    paths:
7	      - 'SOURCES/**'
8	      - 'GOVERNMENTS/**'
9	      - 'TOPICS/**'

11	jobs:
12	  preserve:
13	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
14	    permissions:
15	      contents: write
16	
17	    steps:
18	      - name: Checkout vault
19	        uses: actions/checkout@v6
20	        with:
21	          fetch-depth: 2  # Need previous commit to diff
22	
23	      - name: Setup vault environment
24	        uses: ./.github/actions/setup-vault
25	
26	      - name: Extract new URLs from changed notes
27	        id: extract
28	        run: |
29	          # Get files changed in this push
30	          git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD -- '*.md' > /tmp/changed_files.txt
31	          echo "Changed files:"
32	          cat /tmp/changed_files.txt
33	
34	          # Extract URL fields from changed files
35	          python3 - << 'PYTHON'
36	          import re, sys
37	          from pathlib import Path
38	
39	          changed = Path("/tmp/changed_files.txt").read_text().splitlines()
40	          urls = []
41	          for f in changed:
42	              p = Path(f)
43	              if not p.exists() or p.suffix != ".md":
44	                  continue
45	              content = p.read_text(errors="replace")
46	              m = re.search(r"^URL:\s*(.+)$", content, re.MULTILINE)
47	              if m:
48	                  url = m.group(1).strip()
49	                  if url and "web.archive.org" not in url and url != "null":
50	                      urls.append(url)
51	
52	          Path("/tmp/new_urls.txt").write_text("\n".join(urls))
53	          print(f"Found {len(urls)} new URLs to preserve")
54	          for u in urls:
55	              print(f"  {u}")
56	          PYTHON
57	
58	      - name: Submit to Save Page Now
59	        run: |
60	          python3 - << 'PYTHON'
61	          import urllib.request, urllib.error, time
62	          from pathlib import Path
63	
64	          UA = "IDAHO-VAULT/1.0 (Idaho Reports journalism archive; github.com/loganfinney27/IDAHO-VAULT)"
65	          urls_file = Path("/tmp/new_urls.txt")
66	          if not urls_file.exists():
67	              print("No URLs file found, skipping")
68	              exit(0)
69	
70	          urls = [u.strip() for u in urls_file.read_text().splitlines() if u.strip()]
71	          if not urls:
72	              print("No new URLs to preserve")
73	              exit(0)
74	
75	          print(f"Submitting {len(urls)} URLs to Save Page Now...")
76	          results = []
77	          for url in urls:
78	              save_url = f"https://web.archive.org/save/{url}"
79	              try:
80	                  req = urllib.request.Request(
81	                      save_url,
82	                      method="GET",
83	                      headers={"User-Agent": UA, "Accept": "application/json"}
84	                  )
85	                  with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as resp:
86	                      loc = resp.headers.get("Content-Location", "")
87	                      archived = f"https://web.archive.org{loc}" if loc else save_url
88	                      print(f"  ✅ {url[:70]}")
89	                      print(f"     → {archived}")
90	                      results.append((url, archived, True))
91	              except Exception as e:
92	                  print(f"  ❌ {url[:70]}: {e}")
93	                  results.append((url, None, False))
94	              time.sleep(5)  # SPN rate limit
95	
96	          # Write preservation log
97	          from pathlib import Path
98	          from datetime import datetime, timezone
99	          import os
100	
101	          date_str = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
102	          admin = Path("!")
103	          admin.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
104	          log_path = admin / f"wayback-preserve-{date_str}.md"
105	
106	          lines = [
107	              f"# Wayback Preservation Log — {date_str}",
108	              "",
109	              f"URLs submitted to Save Page Now on push to main.",
110	              "",
111	              "| URL | Archived | Status |",
112	              "|---|---|---|",
113	          ]
114	          for url, archived, ok in results:
115	              status = "✅" if ok else "❌"
116	              arc = f"[snapshot]({archived})" if archived else "failed"
117	              lines.append(f"| {url[:70]} | {arc} | {status} |")
118	
119	          log_path.write_text("\n".join(lines), encoding="utf-8")
120	          print(f"\nLog written to {log_path}")
121	          PYTHON
122	
123	      - name: Commit preservation log
124	        run: |
125	          git add "!/"
126	          if ! git diff --cached --quiet; then
127	            git commit -m "wayback preservation log $(date -u +%Y-%m-%d)"
128	            git push
129	          else
130	            echo "Nothing to commit"
131	          fi

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""
3	Linear → Research Brief Generator (Stage 1 Mesh)
4	=================================================
5	Reads a Linear issue payload and generates a Markdown research brief
6	using an LLM API. Output lands in !/ (the vault mailroom) for Logan
7	to review and route.
8	
9	Guardrails:
10	  - Hardcoded output directory: !/
11	  - Only writes files matching BRIEF-*.md
12	  - Max output: 50 KB
13	  - No shell commands, no file deletion, no path traversal
14	  - Fails gracefully if no API key is set
15	
16	Usage:
17	  # From environment (GitHub Actions)
18	  LINEAR_PAYLOAD='{"title":"...","id":"VAULT-42"}' python3 linear_brief_generator.py
19	
20	  # From CLI (local testing)
21	  python3 linear_brief_generator.py --payload '{"title":"...","id":"VAULT-42"}'
22	
23	  # Dry run (print to stdout, don't write file)
24	  python3 linear_brief_generator.py --dry-run --payload '...'
25	"""
26	
27	import argparse
28	import json
29	import os
30	import sys
31	from datetime import datetime, timezone
32	from pathlib import Path
33	
34	# ── Guardrails ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
35	
36	VAULT_ROOT = Path(".")
37	ALLOWED_OUTPUT_DIR = VAULT_ROOT / "!"
38	ALLOWED_PREFIX = "BRIEF-"
39	MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES = 50 * 1024  # 50 KB
40	OPENAI_MODEL = os.environ.get("OPENAI_MODEL", "gpt-5.4")
41	OPENAI_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS = int(os.environ.get("OPENAI_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS", "4096"))
42	
43	# ── LLM Prompt ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
44	
45	SYSTEM_PROMPT = """\
46	You are a research assistant for a journalism vault. Given a Linear issue
47	describing a story or investigation, produce a structured research brief
48	in Markdown. The brief should include:
49	
50	1. **Summary** — What is this about? (2-3 sentences)
51	2. **Key Questions** — What needs to be answered? (bulleted list)
52	3. **Known Sources** — Who or what should be consulted? (bulleted list)
53	4. **Background** — Relevant context, prior reporting, or public records
54	5. **Next Steps** — Concrete actions to advance the story
55	
56	Be specific to Idaho journalism. Cite public records, state agencies,
57	and legislative processes where relevant. Do not fabricate sources or
58	quotes. If you don't know something, say so.
59	"""
60	
61	
62	def build_user_prompt(payload: dict) -> str:
63	    """Build the user prompt from Linear issue fields."""
64	    parts = []
65	    if payload.get("title"):
66	        parts.append(f"**Issue:** {payload['title']}")
67	    if payload.get("description"):
68	        parts.append(f"**Description:**\n{payload['description']}")
69	    if payload.get("labels"):
70	        labels = payload["labels"]
71	        if isinstance(labels, list):
72	            label_names = [lb.get("name", str(lb)) if isinstance(lb, dict) else str(lb) for lb in labels]
73	            parts.append(f"**Labels:** {', '.join(label_names)}")
74	    if payload.get("priority"):
75	        parts.append(f"**Priority:** {payload['priority']}")
76	    if payload.get("assignee"):
77	        assignee = payload["assignee"]
78	        name = assignee.get("name", str(assignee)) if isinstance(assignee, dict) else str(assignee)
79	        parts.append(f"**Assignee:** {name}")
80	    return "\n\n".join(parts) if parts else "No issue details provided."
81	
82	
83	# ── LLM Providers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
84	
85	def call_anthropic(system: str, user: str) -> str:
86	    """Call Claude API via the Anthropic SDK."""
87	    import anthropic
88	    client = anthropic.Anthropic()
89	    message = client.messages.create(
90	        model="claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
91	        max_tokens=4096,
92	        system=system,
93	        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": user}],
94	    )
95	    return message.content[0].text
96	
97	
98	def call_openai(system: str, user: str) -> str:
99	    """Call OpenAI Responses API via the official OpenAI SDK."""
100	    from openai import OpenAI
101	
102	    client = OpenAI()
103	    response = client.responses.create(
104	        model=OPENAI_MODEL,
105	        max_output_tokens=OPENAI_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS,
106	        input=[
107	            {
108	                "role": "system",
109	                "content": [{"type": "input_text", "text": system}],
110	            },
111	            {
112	                "role": "user",
113	                "content": [{"type": "input_text", "text": user}],
114	            },
115	        ],
116	    )
117	
118	    if response.output_text:
119	        return response.output_text
120	
121	    raise RuntimeError("OpenAI response did not include output_text.")
122	
123	
124	def call_llm(system: str, user: str) -> str:
125	    """Try available LLM providers in order."""
126	    if os.environ.get("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"):
127	        return call_anthropic(system, user)
128	    if os.environ.get("OPENAI_API_KEY"):
129	        return call_openai(system, user)
130	    print("ERROR: No LLM API key found.", file=sys.stderr)
131	    print("  Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or OPENAI_API_KEY.", file=sys.stderr)
132	    sys.exit(1)
133	
134	
135	# ── Output ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
136	
137	def build_frontmatter(payload: dict, date_str: str) -> str:
138	    """Build YAML frontmatter for the brief."""
139	    linear_id = payload.get("id", payload.get("identifier", "UNKNOWN"))
140	    title = payload.get("title", "Untitled")
141	    lines = [
142	        "---",
143	        f"title: \"BRIEF — {title}\"",
144	        f"linear_id: \"{linear_id}\"",
145	        f"date: {date_str}",
146	        "status: draft",
147	        "type: brief",
148	        "source: Linear",
149	        "generated: true",
150	        "---",
151	    ]
152	    return "\n".join(lines)
153	
154	
155	def safe_output_path(payload: dict, date_str: str) -> Path:
156	    """Build a safe output path. Rejects path traversal."""
157	    linear_id = payload.get("id", payload.get("identifier", "UNKNOWN"))
158	    # Sanitize: only allow alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores
159	    safe_id = "".join(c for c in str(linear_id) if c.isalnum() or c in "-_")
160	    if not safe_id:
161	        safe_id = "UNKNOWN"
162	
163	    filename = f"{ALLOWED_PREFIX}{safe_id}-{date_str}.md"
164	
165	    # Belt-and-suspenders: reject traversal
166	    if ".." in filename or "/" in filename or "\\" in filename:
167	        print(f"ERROR: Unsafe filename: {filename}", file=sys.stderr)
168	        sys.exit(1)
169	
170	    return ALLOWED_OUTPUT_DIR / filename
171	
172	
173	# ── Main ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
174	
175	def main() -> int:
176	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Generate research brief from Linear issue")
177	    parser.add_argument("--payload", type=str, help="Linear issue JSON (alternative to LINEAR_PAYLOAD env)")
178	    parser.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true", help="Print to stdout instead of writing file")
179	    args = parser.parse_args()
180	
181	    # Get payload
182	    raw = args.payload or os.environ.get("LINEAR_PAYLOAD", "")
183	    if not raw:
184	        print("ERROR: No payload provided.", file=sys.stderr)
185	        print("  Use --payload or set LINEAR_PAYLOAD env var.", file=sys.stderr)
186	        return 1
187	
188	    try:
189	        payload = json.loads(raw)
190	    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
191	        print(f"ERROR: Invalid JSON payload: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
192	        return 1
193	
194	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
195	    date_str = now.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
196	
197	    # Build prompts
198	    user_prompt = build_user_prompt(payload)
199	    print(f"Generating brief for: {payload.get('title', 'Untitled')}")
200	
201	    # Call LLM
202	    brief_body = call_llm(SYSTEM_PROMPT, user_prompt)
203	
204	    # Assemble output
205	    frontmatter = build_frontmatter(payload, date_str)
206	    output = f"{frontmatter}\n\n{brief_body}\n"
207	
208	    # Size check
209	    output_bytes = len(output.encode("utf-8"))
210	    if output_bytes > MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES:
211	        print(f"ERROR: Output too large ({output_bytes} bytes > {MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES} limit)", file=sys.stderr)
212	        return 1
213	
214	    if args.dry_run:
215	        print("--- DRY RUN ---")
216	        print(output)
217	        print(f"--- {output_bytes} bytes ---")
218	        return 0
219	
220	    # Write file
221	    out_path = safe_output_path(payload, date_str)
222	    ALLOWED_OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
223	    out_path.write_text(output, encoding="utf-8")
224	    print(f"Brief written to {out_path} ({output_bytes} bytes)")
225	    return 0
226	
227	
228	if __name__ == "__main__":
229	    sys.exit(main())
230

1	"""Classify changed file paths by risk tier for auto-PR decisions.
2	
3	Reads file paths from stdin (one per line) and outputs a JSON object:
4	  {"tier": "high"|"low", "high_risk_files": [...], "low_risk_files": [...]}
5	
6	Rule: if ANY file is high-risk, the entire result is high-risk.
7	Unknown paths default to high-risk (fail-safe).
8	"""
9	
10	import json
11	import sys
12	
13	HIGH_RISK_PREFIXES = (
14	    "!/",
15	    ".github/workflows/",
16	    ".github/scripts/",
17	)
18	
19	HIGH_RISK_EXACT = {
20	    "CLAUDE.md",
21	    ".gitignore",
22	    ".github/copilot-instructions.md",
23	    # Note: governance files under !/ are covered by HIGH_RISK_PREFIXES ("!/")
24	    # Old !ADMIN/ and !ADMINISTRATION/ paths removed — those folders no longer exist
25	}
26	
27	LOW_RISK_PREFIXES = (
28	    "SOURCES/",
29	    "TOPICS/",
30	    "PEOPLE/",
31	    "PLACES/",
32	    "ORGANIZATIONS/",
33	    "GOVERNMENTS/",
34	    "ATTACHMENTS/",
35	    "X LABELER/",
36	    ".github/swarm/",
37	    "!/swarm/",
38	)
39	
40	
41	def classify(path: str) -> str:
42	    """Return 'high' or 'low' for a single file path."""
43	    if path in HIGH_RISK_EXACT:
44	        return "high"
45	    for prefix in HIGH_RISK_PREFIXES:
46	        if path.startswith(prefix):
47	            return "high"
48	    for prefix in LOW_RISK_PREFIXES:
49	        if path.startswith(prefix):
50	            return "low"
51	    # Unknown path → high risk (fail-safe)
52	    return "high"
53	
54	
55	def main():
56	    paths = [line.strip() for line in sys.stdin if line.strip()]
57	    if not paths:
58	        print(json.dumps({"tier": "low", "high_risk_files": [], "low_risk_files": []}))
59	        return
60	
61	    high_risk = []
62	    low_risk = []
63	
64	    for p in paths:
65	        if classify(p) == "high":
66	            high_risk.append(p)
67	        else:
68	            low_risk.append(p)
69	
70	    tier = "high" if high_risk else "low"
71	    print(json.dumps({
72	        "tier": tier,
73	        "high_risk_files": high_risk,
74	        "low_risk_files": low_risk,
75	    }))
76	
77	
78	if __name__ == "__main__":
79	    main()
80

2	"""
3	Post a legislative activity comment to the pinned Daily Digest GitHub Issue.
4	
5	This script is called by the scraper workflow after bills are committed.
6	It finds (or creates) the pinned digest issue, then appends a dated comment
7	summarising what changed. GitHub sends a notification to all subscribers,
8	making it readable on GitHub mobile.
9	
10	Requires:
11	  - GH_TOKEN env var (automatically set in GitHub Actions)
12	  - gh CLI (pre-installed on GitHub Actions ubuntu-latest runners)
13	  - GITHUB_REPOSITORY env var (set automatically by GitHub Actions)
14	"""
15	
16	import argparse
17	import json
18	import os
19	import re
20	import subprocess
21	import sys
22	from datetime import datetime, timezone
23	from pathlib import Path
24	
25	DIGEST_ISSUE_TITLE = "📋 Idaho Legislature — Daily Activity Digest"
26	DIGEST_LABEL       = "daily-digest"
27	VAULT_ROOT         = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
28	BILLS_DIR          = VAULT_ROOT / "GOVERNMENTS" / "IDAHO - LEGISLATIVE" / "BILLS"
29	
30	
31	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
32	# gh CLI helpers
33	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
34	
35	def gh(*args: str, check: bool = True) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess:
36	    """Run a gh CLI command; return the CompletedProcess."""
37	    return subprocess.run(
38	        ["gh", *args],
39	        capture_output=True, text=True, check=check,
40	    )
41	
42	
43	def gh_json(*args: str) -> dict | list | None:
44	    """Run gh and parse JSON output. Returns None on failure."""
45	    try:
46	        result = gh(*args)
47	        return json.loads(result.stdout)
48	    except (subprocess.CalledProcessError, json.JSONDecodeError):
49	        return None
50	
51	
52	def ensure_label() -> None:
53	    """Create the daily-digest label if it doesn't exist."""
54	    repo = os.environ.get("GITHUB_REPOSITORY", "")
55	    existing = gh_json("label", "list", "--repo", repo, "--json", "name", "--limit", "100")
56	    if existing and any(l["name"] == DIGEST_LABEL for l in existing):
57	        return
58	    gh("label", "create", DIGEST_LABEL,
59	       "--color", "0075ca",
60	       "--description", "Auto-posted daily Idaho Legislature activity summary",
61	       "--repo", repo,
62	       check=False)
63	
64	
65	def find_or_create_digest_issue() -> int:
66	    """Return the issue number of the digest issue, creating it if needed."""
67	    repo = os.environ.get("GITHUB_REPOSITORY", "")
68	
69	    # Search for open issue with the digest title
70	    issues = gh_json(
71	        "issue", "list",
72	        "--repo", repo,
73	        "--label", DIGEST_LABEL,
74	        "--state", "open",
75	        "--json", "number,title",
76	        "--limit", "5",
77	    )
78	
79	    if issues:
80	        for issue in issues:
81	            if issue["title"] == DIGEST_ISSUE_TITLE:
82	                return issue["number"]
83	
84	    # Not found — create it
85	    body = (
86	        "This issue receives a comment every time the Idaho Legislature scraper "
87	        "detects bill activity. **Subscribe** (Watch → Custom → Issues) to get "
88	        "a push notification on GitHub mobile each morning.\n\n"
89	        "- Trigger a manual scrape: **Actions → Idaho Legislature Scraper → Run workflow**\n"
90	        "- Each comment below is one day's legislative activity.\n"
91	        "- Bills are stored in `GOVERNMENTS/IDAHO - LEGISLATIVE/BILLS/`\n"
92	    )
93	    result = gh(
94	        "issue", "create",
95	        "--repo",  repo,
96	        "--title", DIGEST_ISSUE_TITLE,
97	        "--label", DIGEST_LABEL,
98	        "--body",  body,
99	    )
100	    # Parse issue number from URL in stdout (e.g. "https://github.com/org/repo/issues/42")
101	    url = result.stdout.strip()
102	    m = re.search(r'/issues/(\d+)$', url)
103	    if m:
104	        return int(m.group(1))
105	
106	    # Fallback: re-query
107	    issues = gh_json(
108	        "issue", "list",
109	        "--repo", repo,
110	        "--label", DIGEST_LABEL,
111	        "--state", "open",
112	        "--json", "number,title",
113	        "--limit", "5",
114	    )
115	    if issues:
116	        return issues[0]["number"]
117	
118	    print("ERROR: Could not find or create digest issue", file=sys.stderr)
119	    sys.exit(1)
120	
121	
122	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
123	# Bill file reader
124	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
125	
126	def read_bill_summary(filepath: Path) -> dict:
127	    """
128	    Extract key fields from a bill markdown file for the digest.
129	    Returns dict: {alias, title, last_action, url}
130	    """
131	    if not filepath.exists():
132	        return {}
133	    text = filepath.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
134	
135	    alias = ""
136	    url   = ""
137	    title = ""
138	
139	    # Parse frontmatter
140	    fm_match = re.match(r'^---\n(.+?)\n---', text, re.DOTALL)
141	    if fm_match:
142	        fm = fm_match.group(1)
143	        alias_m = re.search(r'^  - (.+)$', fm, re.M)
144	        if alias_m:
145	            alias = alias_m.group(1).strip()
146	        url_m = re.search(r'^URL:\s*(.+)$', fm, re.M)
147	        if url_m:
148	            url = url_m.group(1).strip()
149	
150	    # Pull first substantive body line as the title/description
151	    body = text[fm_match.end():].strip() if fm_match else text
152	    for line in body.splitlines():
153	        line = line.strip()
154	        if line and not line.startswith(">") and not line.startswith("by "):
155	            title = line[:120]
156	            break
157	
158	    # Pull last history entry as the most recent action
159	    last_action = ""
160	    for line in reversed(body.splitlines()):
161	        m = re.match(r'^(\d{1,2}/\d{1,2})\s+\t(.+)$', line)
162	        if m:
163	            last_action = f"{m.group(1)}: {m.group(2)}"
164	            break
165	
166	    # Fall back to "Last action:" line
167	    if not last_action:
168	        m = re.search(r'Last action:\s*(.+)', body)
169	        if m:
170	            last_action = m.group(1).strip()
171	
172	    return {
173	        "alias":       alias or filepath.stem,
174	        "title":       title,
175	        "last_action": last_action,
176	        "url":         url,
177	    }
178	
179	
180	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
181	# Comment builder
182	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
183	
184	def build_comment(
185	    changed_paths: list[str],
186	    new_paths: list[str],
187	    year: str,
188	) -> str:
189	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
190	    date_str = now.strftime("%A, %B %-d, %Y")   # e.g. "Thursday, March 12, 2026"
191	    time_str = now.strftime("%H:%M UTC")
192	
193	    new_set     = set(new_paths)
194	    updated_set = set(changed_paths) - new_set
195	
196	    lines = [f"## {date_str} — Idaho Legislature Activity"]
197	    lines.append(f"*Scraped at {time_str} · {year} Regular Session*")
198	    lines.append("")
199	
200	    def bill_row(path_str: str) -> str:
201	        p = Path(path_str)
202	        info = read_bill_summary(BILLS_DIR / p.name if not p.is_absolute() else p)
203	        alias = info.get("alias", p.stem)
204	        title = info.get("title", "")
205	        action = info.get("last_action", "")
206	        url   = info.get("url", "")
207	        link  = f"[{alias}]({url})" if url else alias
208	        title_short = (title[:80] + "…") if len(title) > 80 else title
209	        return f"| {link} | {title_short} | {action} |"
210	
211	    if new_set:
212	        lines.append(f"### 🆕 New bills introduced ({len(new_set)})")
213	        lines.append("")
214	        lines.append("| Bill | Description | Status |")
215	        lines.append("|------|-------------|--------|")
216	        for p in sorted(new_set):
217	            lines.append(bill_row(p))
218	        lines.append("")
219	
220	    if updated_set:
221	        lines.append(f"### 🔄 Bills with activity ({len(updated_set)})")
222	        lines.append("")
223	        lines.append("| Bill | Description | Latest Action |")
224	        lines.append("|------|-------------|---------------|")
225	        for p in sorted(updated_set):
226	            lines.append(bill_row(p))
227	        lines.append("")
228	
229	    total = len(new_set) + len(updated_set)
230	    lines.append(f"*{total} bill file(s) updated in this run.*")
231	
232	    return "\n".join(lines)
233	
234	
235	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
236	# Entry point
237	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
238	
239	def main() -> int:
240	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
241	        description="Post a daily legislative digest comment to a GitHub Issue."
242	    )
243	    parser.add_argument("--changed-file", required=True,
244	                        help="File listing paths of changed bill files (one per line)")
245	    parser.add_argument("--new-file", required=True,
246	                        help="File listing paths of newly created bill files (one per line)")
247	    parser.add_argument("--year", default="2026")
248	    args = parser.parse_args()
249	
250	    changed_paths = Path(args.changed_file).read_text().splitlines()
251	    new_paths     = Path(args.new_file).read_text().splitlines()
252	    changed_paths = [p.strip() for p in changed_paths if p.strip()]
253	    new_paths     = [p.strip() for p in new_paths     if p.strip()]
254	
255	    if not changed_paths and not new_paths:
256	        print("No bill changes — skipping digest comment.")
257	        return 0
258	
259	    ensure_label()
260	    issue_number = find_or_create_digest_issue()
261	    comment = build_comment(changed_paths, new_paths, args.year)
262	
263	    repo = os.environ.get("GITHUB_REPOSITORY", "")
264	    result = gh(
265	        "issue", "comment", str(issue_number),
266	        "--repo", repo,
267	        "--body", comment,
268	    )
269	    print(f"Posted digest to issue #{issue_number}")
270	    print(result.stdout.strip())
271	    return 0
272	
273	
274	if __name__ == "__main__":
275	    sys.exit(main())
276
